select-distinct.label: Removing Duplicates with DISTINCT
select-distinct.summary: How to use DISTINCT to remove duplicate rows.

sk1.title: What is DISTINCT?
sk1.bodyMarkdown: When you use the `SELECT` statement in SQL, you may get duplicate rows if the same value appears more than once in a column. The `DISTINCT` keyword helps you return only unique values, removing any duplicates from your results.

sk2.title: How to Use DISTINCT
sk2.bodyMarkdown:
To use `DISTINCT`, place it right after `SELECT` and before the column name(s). For example:

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT city FROM customers;
```
This query returns each city only once, even if multiple customers are from the same city.

sk3.title: DISTINCT with Multiple Columns
sk3.bodyMarkdown:
You can use `DISTINCT` with more than one column. In this case, SQL returns unique combinations of the selected columns. For example:

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT city, country FROM customers;
```
This query returns each unique pair of city and country.

q1.title: What does the DISTINCT keyword do in a SELECT statement?

options.c: It filters rows based on a condition.

q2.title: Which of the following queries will return only unique cities from the customers table?

options.c: SELECT DISTINCT city, country FROM customers;

q3.title: Put the steps in order to write a DISTINCT query.
q3.prompt: Drag the steps into the correct order.

q4.title: Write a query to list all unique product categories from the products table.
q4.prompt: Use the products_catalog dataset. Show each category only once.
